---
title: "My report"
author: "Zane"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
1 + 1
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).

## My report section

The number of rows in the iris dataset is `r nrow(iris)`

```{r}
plot(iris$Sepal.Length, iris$Petal.Length)
```

Let's do some summary statistics.

```{r}
#| echo: false

my_model \<- lm(iris$Petal.Length ~ iris$Sepal.Length)

my_summary \<- summary(my_model)

r2 \<- my_summary\$adj.r.square
```

The $R^2$ for the linear model that I fit was `r round(r2, 2)`.

$\hat{y}=\hat{\beta}_0 + \hat{\beta}_1$

My equation for this line is:

$$
y = \beta_0 + \beta_1Sex+\beta_2Time +\epsilon
$$

$R^2_1$

\$ \displaystyle\sum\_{i=1}\^{23} x\^2 \$

We can calculate the integral

$$
\int_{\mathbb{R}} x \ dx \ \text{ where } x is some number
$$
